frameworks.name: Original GraphDeco/Inria gaussian-splatting
frameworks.reason: License restricts use to non-commercial research/evaluation unless explicit consent is granted.
commercialConditions: Do not import, vendor, run, train with, or derive product runtime code from this implementation without explicit commercial permission from the licensors.

frameworks.name: gsplat
frameworks.reason: Permissive license and suitable low-level backend for Gaussian splatting experiments.
commercialConditions: Keep Apache-2.0 license text and notices when distributing code/binaries.
Review transitive Python/CUDA dependencies before product packaging.

frameworks.name: Nerfstudio Splatfacto
frameworks.reason: Good first training path that uses gsplat and can export Gaussian splats.
commercialConditions: Keep Apache-2.0 license text and notices when distributing code/binaries.
Record exact Nerfstudio version and export path used for production artifacts.
Review transitive dependencies before packaging.

frameworks.name: COLMAP
frameworks.reason: Mature SfM command-line tool; third-party dependency licenses must be recorded per install/build.
commercialConditions: Keep COLMAP license and copyright notices.
Record install/build source and third-party dependency license set for the exact binary used.

frameworks.name: FFmpeg / ffprobe
frameworks.license: LGPL-2.1-or-later, GPL if GPL components are enabled, nonfree if nonfree components are enabled
frameworks.condition: Use system binary for lab; record `ffmpeg -version`; do not bundle or ship until build flags and codec/patent concerns are reviewed.
commercialConditions: Use system-installed ffmpeg/ffprobe for lab and local RTX workstation runs.
Record ffmpeg -version and configure flags in every environment report.
Do not bundle or redistribute FFmpeg until LGPL/GPL/nonfree build flags and codec patent exposure are reviewed.
If distributed, provide required notices and source-offer/compliance material.

frameworks.name: PyTorch
frameworks.reason: Permissive-style license; required by gsplat/Nerfstudio stack.
commercialConditions: Keep license and notices when distributing.
Review binary package dependency notices for the exact wheel/container used.

frameworks.name: TorchVision
frameworks.role: PyTorch vision utilities; installed with the CUDA smoke environment for compatibility with later training/viewer tooling
frameworks.reason: PyTorch ecosystem package installed from the official PyTorch CUDA wheel index; keep notices and review transitive dependencies before packaging.
commercialConditions: Keep license and notices when distributing.
Review binary package dependency notices for the exact wheel/container used.
Do not use bundled sample datasets or model weights unless their separate licenses allow the intended use.

frameworks.name: Python packaging
frameworks.role: Python package version and compatibility utility used by the training runtime stack
frameworks.reason: Permissive dual-license metadata package required by local torch/gsplat training execution.
commercialConditions: Keep Apache-2.0 or BSD-2-Clause license notices when distributing.
Record the exact installed version in the Python environment freeze.

frameworks.name: NVIDIA CUDA Toolkit/driver
frameworks.condition: Allowed on the local RTX workstation; do not redistribute CUDA components unless NVIDIA terms explicitly allow the intended distribution.
commercialConditions: Allowed for local RTX workstation use under NVIDIA terms.
Do not redistribute CUDA Toolkit, driver files, or CUDA runtime components unless the current NVIDIA EULA permits the exact redistribution path.
Record driver, CUDA toolkit and container versions in environment reports.

frameworks.name: Spark
frameworks.role: preferred production-style browser Gaussian Splat viewer spike after the current point-debug UI
frameworks.reason: Active Three.js-based renderer with support for PLY, SPZ, SPLAT, KSPLAT and SOG formats; latest checked package version is 2.1.0.
commercialConditions: Keep MIT license and copyright notice in distributed app or notices bundle.
Review transitive npm dependency licenses before shipping.

frameworks.name: Three.js
frameworks.reason: Permissive license and broad browser 3D ecosystem support.
commercialConditions: Keep MIT license and copyright notice in distributed app or notices bundle.

frameworks.name: SuperSplat open-source components
frameworks.license: MIT for Editor, Viewer and splat-transform; hosted platform proprietary
frameworks.reason: Self-hosted open-source components are permissive; hosted publishing platform stays outside the core pipeline.
commercialConditions: Use only open-source MIT components that are present in the repo/license review.
Do not depend on hosted PlayCanvas publishing/platform terms for the core pipeline unless separately approved.
Keep MIT notices for distributed components.

frameworks.name: GaussianSplats3D
frameworks.reason: Permissive, but maintainer notes the repo is no longer active. Keep as fallback, not first choice.
commercialConditions: Keep MIT license and copyright notice.
Use only as fallback because the project is not active.

frameworks.name: OpenSplat
frameworks.reason: Commercial use is possible under AGPL, but AGPL obligations are not accepted for this product path by default.
commercialConditions: Do not use in this product path unless AGPL obligations are explicitly accepted or a separate commercial license is obtained.

frameworks.name: React
frameworks.reason: Permissive license and familiar frontend stack.
commercialConditions: Keep MIT license and copyright notice in distributed app or notices bundle.
Review npm dependency tree before shipping.

frameworks.name: Vite
frameworks.reason: Permissive license and simple local dev/build workflow.
commercialConditions: Keep MIT license and copyright notice if distributed.
Review npm dependency tree before shipping.

frameworks.name: FastAPI
frameworks.role: future local API server if CLI is not enough
frameworks.reason: Useful later, but not needed until the first CLI pipeline stages are real.
commercialConditions: Keep MIT license and copyright notice if distributed.
Review Python dependency tree before shipping.

commercialUsePolicy.goal: Pipeline candidates must be usable for possible commercial product evaluation before they can become product runtime dependencies.
commercialUsePolicy.productionRule: No framework is production-ready until commercialUse is non-blocked, conditions are satisfied, exact version/source is recorded, license text/notices are collected, and transitive dependencies are reviewed.
commercialUsePolicy.artifactRule: Generated splats inherit obligations from the input media and any model/tool license terms that apply to generated output; every production candidate must record source rights for the video and resulting artifact.
